body: PT MNC Land Tbk has sold assets worth IDR 5.5 trillion to PT Bumi Indah Permai for the Bali Subway project, expected to be completed by January 2025, which aims to reduce traffic congestion and boost Bali's economy. KPig, the company behind the project, has reported revenue of IDR 1.26 trillion and net profit of IDR 553 billion as of September 2024, according to IDNFinancials.com data.
title: MNC Land sells IDR 5.5 trillion assets to Bumi Indah Permai for Bali subway project.

body: ESSA SR (no ticker) owns 99.9% of ESI's issued paid-up capital of IDR 10,001,000,000 through direct and indirect ownership, with each ESI share valued at IDR 1 million. ESSA SR has established a new subsidiary, PT ESSA SAF MAKMUR, with a paid-up capital of IDR 10,001,000,000, which is expected to improve support for ESSA SR's operational activities without impacting its operational, legal, or financial condition.
title: ESSA Establishes New Subsidiary PT ESSA Saf Makmur Through ESI to Support Operational Activities

body: PT Estika Tata Tiara TBK (ETTT) recorded a surge in revenue in September 2024, reaching IDR 2.64 trillion, a 589% increase from the same period last year, driven mainly by its distribution and sales segment which contributed IDR 2.63 trillion. The company's revenue growth was also driven by its diversified business, including live cattle, meat, makloon, and slaughter services, with key products such as sausage, murato, and saikoro contributing significantly to its total revenue.
title: PT Estika Tata Tiara TBK Sees 589% Revenue Surge in September 2024

body: PT Mulia Boga Raya Tbk (KEJU) will distribute bonus shares worth IDR 206.25 billion, with a ratio of 4:11, following approval at the company's extraordinary general meeting of shareholders. The company's share price has surged 92.17% or 1,060 points since the beginning of the year, closing at IDR 2,210 per share, up 1.38% or 30 points in the first trading session today.
title: PT Mulia Boga Raya to Distribute IDR 206.25 Billion in Bonus Shares to Shareholders.

body: The Jakarta Composite Stock Price Index (JCI) rose 1.22% or 85.13 points to 7,068.99 in the first trading session, with a total transaction value of IDR 5.05 trillion and 597,680 transactions. PT Era Digital Media Tbk (ERAD) was the most traded stock, while PT Agro Yasa Lestari Tbk (AYLS) was the top gainer with a 32.05% price increase, and PT Sekar Bumi Tbk (SBUM) was the top loser with a 15% price decline.
title: Jakarta Composite Stock Price Index Rises 1.22% to 7,068.99 in First Trading Session.

body: Foreign investors sold a net 470.06 million shares, with significant sales in Digital Corporation PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO), PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BBRI), and PT Bank Central Asia Tbk (BBCA), among others. Meanwhile, they purchased a significant number of shares in PT Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur Tbk, PT MNC Land Tbk, PT Bumi Resources Tbk, PT XL Axiata Tbk, and PT Merdeka Battery Materials Tbk, with net volumes ranging from 23.49 million to 105.41 million shares.
title: Foreign investors sell 4.10 billion shares, led by Goto Gojek Tokopedia, and buy 3.63 billion shares, led by Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur.

body: PT Raja Roti Cemerlang TBK (BRRC) is set to raise up to IDR 61.2 billion in its initial public offering (IPO) of 291.5 million shares, or 30.01% of its total issued and paid-up capital, at a price range of IDR 200-210 per share. The company will allocate all proceeds from the IPO to working capital, with the offering period taking place from December 18-20, 2024, and listing on the Indonesia Stock Exchange on January 9, 2024.
title: Raja Roti Cemerlang to Raise Up to IDR 61.2 Billion in IPO with 291.5 Million Shares Offered.

body: PT Sinar Mas Multiartha Tbk (TICKER) will issue shelf registration bonds worth IDR 800 billion from January 10-14, 2025, with proceeds mainly used to repay bonds (IDR 575 billion) and the remainder for working capital financing (IDR 225 billion). The bonds will be offered in three series with varying interest rates and terms, with PT Aldiracita Sekuritas Indonesia and PT Sinarmas Sekuritas acting as underwriters.
title: PT Sinar Mas Multiartha to Issue IDR 800 Billion Bonds to Repay Debt and Finance Working Capital

body: PT BPD Jawa Barat dan Banten Tbk (BJBR) has injected IDR 221.40 billion into BPD Jambi to develop its bank business group, which is expected to positively impact dividend income and increase fee-based income through business synergies and inorganic asset growth. Following the capital injection, BJBR will consolidate BPD Jambi's financial statements into its reports, and BPD Jambi's capital shares have increased by 1.05 million shares to 1,072,349 shares.
title: PT BPD Jawa Barat dan Banten Injects IDR 221.40 Billion into BPD Jambi to Strengthen Bank Business Group.

body: PT Bank Mandiri Tbk (BMRI) and PT Bank Central Asia Tbk (BBCA) have consistently ranked as top laggards, contributing to the decline of the Jakarta Composite Index (JCI) for three consecutive days, with BMRI's stock price dropping 2.06% to IDR 5,675 per share and BBCA's falling 1.28% to IDR 9,675. Since the beginning of the year, BMRI's stock has dropped 7.35% to IDR 542.37 trillion in market capitalization, while BBCA's stock has shown a 2.65% gain to IDR 1.180.76 trillion.
title: Bank Mandiri and Bank Central Asia shares continue to decline, weighing down Jakarta Composite Index for three consecutive days.

body: The Jakarta Composite Index (JCI) fell 1.84% to 6,977.24 on Thursday, its steepest drop in six days, with a total transaction value of IDR 14.13 trillion and net foreign sell of IDR 944.05 billion, marking the fifth consecutive day of foreign outflows. The decline was led by blue-chip stocks such as PT Bank Mandiri Tbk (BMRI), PT Chandra Asri Pacific Tbk (TPIA), PT Bank Central Asia Tbk (BBCA), and PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BBRI), while PT Bayan Resources Tbk (BYAN), PT Amman Mineral Internasional Tbk (AMMN), PT Pertamina Geothermal Energy Tbk (PGE), and PT Ciputra Development Tbk (CTRA) were among the top gainers.
title: Jakarta Composite Index Falls 1.84% to 6,977.24, Marking Sixth Consecutive Day of Losses.

body: PT Bank Neo Commerce Tbk (BBYB) has outlined its strategic plan 2025, focusing on digital innovation, cost efficiency, and maintaining credit quality to remain competitive in the banking industry, with a loan-to-deposit ratio of 65% and plans to increase low-cost funds through bundling referral programs. Despite economic challenges, including the implementation of 12% VAT, the bank remains optimistic about growth potential and is in discussions with potential new investors to support its future growth, with a goal to divest shares in Akulaku Group over the next five years.
title: Bank Neo Commerce Outlines Strategic Plan 2025, Focusing on Digital Innovation and Cost Efficiency to Remain Competitive.

body: Foreign investors purchased a net 315.52 million shares of PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO), accounting for 40% of overall foreign buy volume, with other notable acquisitions including PT Daya Inti Yasa Tbk (DYYA), PT Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur Tbk (BIPI), and PT Bumi Resources Minerals Tbk (BRMS). Meanwhile, foreigners sold shares of PT Bank Central Asia Tbk (BBCA), PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BBRI), PT Bumi Resources Tbk (BUMI), PT J Resources Asia Pasifik Tbk (JRAS), and PT Barito Pacific Tbk (BRPT), with net sell volumes ranging from -26.49 million to -97.82 million shares.
title: Foreign investors acquire significant stakes in several Indonesian companies, including GoTo, Daya Intiguna Yasa, and Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur.
